expectations.text: [holdout_regression][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: skill improvements for report-editor do not regress a different feature planning flow
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly frames the check as: “ensuring that skill improvements made for the report-editor feature family (BCIN-6709) do not regress a different feature planning flow” and concludes “PASS (no regression detected for cross-feature planning flow)” with rationale that “A different planning flow (embedding/dashboard editor migration-shell/regression flow) is preserved as a distinct, strong coverage cluster… and is not collapsed or lost due to report-editor-centric improvements.”

expectations.text: [holdout_regression][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase holdout
expectations.evidence: execution_notes.md states: “primary phase: holdout” and “did not attempt to execute phase scripts or generate QA-plan drafts… Output is limited to a holdout regression determination.” result.md matches this by providing a holdout regression verdict (PASS) and evidence-based rationale rather than generating phase artifacts.
